creator: frank@dynamical-systems.org
text:
Hello Gabor,

Circumventing the use of the ext2fs kernel module can basically be done. 
The strategy would be to have the contents of the Slackware install 
floppies extracted beforehand and to provide them as a .tar.gz archive for 
public download. Although I'd be willing to host such an archive, the 
question arises if I'm allowed to do so. This means a redistribution of 
binaries which were released under GPL/LGPL terms. Regardless of the fact 
that all the corresponding sources are freely available at slackware.com, 
it's currently not clear to me if I'm going to violate the GPL by offering 
the floppy contents. I'm trying to find out, maybe you can give me a hint? 
I'm not an expert in this field, and any related information is greatly 
appreciated.

Thanks,
Frank
